hotel_name: Gran Meliá Palacio de los Duques
title: Heritage + modern luxury done right
body: From the moment we stepped into the palace courtyard, the staff made us feel expected. The concierge, Javier, remembered our wedding anniversary from booking and had flowers sent to the suite. The rooftop views over Madrid were breathtaking at sunset. The only issue: the service elevator was slow on day 2. Otherwise flawless.

title: Business-friendly but tired
body: Fine for a business trip — location is great for Diagonal meetings and wifi was strong enough for my video calls. But the rooms feel 1990s. The €25/day parking fee caught me off guard — not disclosed at booking. Breakfast was OK not great. Staff professional.

title: Family heaven with small gotchas
body: Kids absolutely loved the Family Concierge service — Alejandra went above and beyond to arrange a private pool day. The buffet had enough variety for picky eaters. My only gripe: the advertised 'connecting rooms' required an upgrade charge of $80/night that was not in the booking email. We paid it but felt hoodwinked.

title: Stylish but too loud by midnight
body: The design, the beach, the people — ME Ibiza is a lifestyle experience. Our room had the best view we'd ever had at a hotel. BUT — the bar noise travels straight through the walls until 2am. If you want to sleep, book the building further from the pool. Also the €48 'resort fee' feels excessive.

title: Great for digital nomads
body: Stayed for 3 weeks working remotely. WiFi measured 120mbps consistently, the desk in the Corner King room was actually usable, and the monthly rate they offered me after week 1 saved me ~40%. Breakfast was small but fresh. Would absolutely come back.

title: Dated but acceptable for the price
body: Booked on a Black Friday deal. The room was clean but the bathroom is 30 years old. Pool was crowded, never got a chair. Breakfast had the same items every day for 7 days. Staff was kind. Would I stay again? Only if the price was below €60/night.

title: Gold tier treated like Platinum
body: Arrived as MeliáRewards Gold and was upgraded to an Executive floor with lounge access — unprompted. The Ambassador-level treatment made me actually consider moving my corporate travel from Marriott. Breakfast in the Lounge was superior to many Hilton Honors Gold lounges I've been to.

title: Honeymoon ruined by ignored requests
body: Told them in writing 3 times this was our honeymoon. Got zero acknowledgment at check-in. Room was facing construction despite 'garden view' booking. Staff at Hadar restaurant asked 3 times if we wanted 'the upgrade menu' pushing €140/pp. We left for Hotel Xcaret on day 4 and requested a refund for remaining nights. Will be disputing with Amex.

title: Restaurant Dos Cielos is worth the stay alone
body: Stayed 2 nights to experience Dos Cielos Madrid. The chef's tasting menu was a once-in-a-year event. The hotel side wasn't as sharp — check-in took 11 minutes because the system was down — but the dining makes it easy to forgive.

title: Family friendly beachfront — mind the buffet chaos
body: Kids loved it. Entertainment team were heroes. BUT — at peak breakfast hours (9-10am) it is absolute chaos, with 20-min queues for eggs. If you can do 7:30 or 10:30 you're fine. Beach location is everything we hoped for.

title: Does what it says on the tin
body: Used as airport overnight before an early flight. Shuttle was on time, room was quiet, bed was comfortable enough. Breakfast was the budget buffet but it was ready at 4am for early-flight guests which mattered more than variety. No complaints for the use case.

title: Advertised family-friendly, is not
body: Booked for 4 days with two kids. The 'family pool' closes at 17:00 (who decided this?). The kids club advertised 9-12 and 15-18 but was actually 10-12 only — the afternoon session was canceled all week without notice. Room bed configuration was also nothing like photos — 1 queen + 1 sofa bed, not 2 queens.

title: Rooftop bar is one of the best views in Athens
body: The location is average — not in Plaka — but the rooftop view of the Acropolis at sunset is world-class. Worth booking just for that. Rooms are small but modern. Service warm and personal. Would return.

title: Hidden Andalusian gem
body: Tucked into the Santa Cruz quarter. The patio breakfast under orange trees was a moment I'll remember. The only con: wifi is spotty in the inner rooms due to the historic walls. Staff helped us reconnect but it was a theme.

title: All-inclusive done right
body: The all-inclusive actually WAS all-inclusive. No 'premium' restaurants requiring extra. Spa was included for 2 treatments. Spirits and wine were quality brands. After three 'all-inclusive' properties that nickel-and-dimed us, this was a revelation.

title: Great design, average service for the price
body: The rooftop pool and hotel design are Instagram-perfect. But for €600/night, the service felt slow and impersonal — we were 'Sir' and 'Madam' all week despite being Platinum. Four Seasons does this better at the same price.

title: Reliable layover hotel
body: One-night layover before a long-haul flight. Shuttle every 30 min, fast check-in, quiet room. Breakfast at 4:30am was genuinely helpful for my 7am flight. Would use again for layovers.

title: Beach is beautiful, rest is tired
body: Can't beat the Varadero beach. But the rooms are falling apart — mold in bathroom, broken AC for 2 of 7 nights despite complaints. Buffet repetitive but acceptable. If Sol Palmeras wasn't on the beach, it wouldn't be worth it.

title: Feels like a London club, not a chain hotel
body: Stayed in a Premium Plus room. The building is classic 1930s and retains character. Staff remembered my coffee order by day 2. Walking distance to Regent's Park. This is the kind of Meliá property that justifies the brand premium.

title: Nice resort but time-share pressure is intense
body: The property itself is beautiful and the staff were lovely. But from day 1 we were pushed into 'hotel orientation' meetings that turned out to be 90-min time-share pitches. Told them no 4 different times. Completely undermined the relaxation factor.

title: Great for trade shows, quiet enough to focus
body: Stayed during Drupa. 15-min walk to Messe, quiet floor on request honored, fast wifi. Breakfast was solid, especially the fresh pastries. Would recommend for business trade show stays.

title: Solid family pick if price is right
body: 7 nights all-inclusive with two kids. Pool area is the highlight, well-maintained and large. Rooms are older but clean. Food buffet had Spanish + international options. The all-inclusive drinks are house-brand only — premium charged separately, which we weren't told at booking.

title: Meliá does Paris respectfully
body: Location is exceptional. The rooms are smaller than US standard but normal for Paris. The breakfast hall has a retractable roof which is charming in April sun. Service was French-formal in a good way.

title: Madison Square Park location at reasonable NY prices
body: Modern design, excellent location 2 blocks from Madison Square Park. Room was small (normal NY). Rooftop bar view was stunning. €420/night felt fair for Manhattan midweek.

title: Entertainment is loud until midnight
body: Came for quiet couple's week. Made the mistake of not asking about entertainment schedule. Live music every night 21:00-23:30 in the central piazza — audible from every room. Staff were apologetic but couldn't change anything.

title: Masterclass in Latin American hospitality
body: The warmth of the staff is unmatched. From check-in to checkout, every interaction felt genuine, not scripted. The asado night at the hotel restaurant is a must. They even arranged a last-minute private tour when our original guide canceled.

title: Solid Vienna choice at a business rate
body: Corporate rate came in at €178, breakfast included, and the Danube view from a 20th floor room was a surprise bonus. Breakfast had classic Austrian pastries. Would book again for Vienna business.

title: Central but dated and overpriced
body: At €220/night I expected more. Room was OK, wifi worked, but the bathroom looks 1998 and the minibar has items priced at 4x supermarket. Central location is the only thing justifying the rate.

title: Honeymoon magic
body: Booked the Reserve tier for our honeymoon. The private section with its own beach, butler service, and restaurant made it feel like we were the only guests. Our butler Carlos knew our preferences by day 2. Worth every penny.

title: Underwhelming after the renovation
body: Stayed 3 years ago and loved it. Came back post-renovation — the new lobby is sterile, rooms have cheaper finishes than before despite the 'upgrade'. Not sure what the 2023 renovation budget went to. Still fine, just not as characterful.
